name: Humión and Mancubo Circular Trail
name_original: Subida a Humión - desde Cubilla de la Sierra — circular por el Parque natural Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil
region: Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina
description: Hike the difficult 9.3-mile (14.9 km) Ascent to Humión circular route from Cubilla de la Sierra for panoramic views and 2075 feet of gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina offers diverse terrain within the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. The region features the Ebro Valley, the dramatic Molinar River gorge with its waterfalls, and the elevated peaks of the Montes Obarenes, including Pico Humión. Extensive beech and pine forests cover much of the landscape, providing varied environments for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina The most popular hiking route is Humión and Mancubo Circular Trail, a 9.3 miles (14.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends towards Pico Humión, offering expansive views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Humion – TalasDelSomo loop from Peña San Pedro, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This trail explores the forested slopes around Pico Humión. Local hikers also love the Frías – Hanging Houses of Frías loop from Frías, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through the historic village of Frías and its unique architecture, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina is defined by river valleys, forested slopes, and mountain ascents within the Montes Obarenes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to challenging mountain trails. The routes in Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina?
A: There are over 80 hiking trails in Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina, offering a wide range of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes can I expect on hikes in this region?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, transitioning from the tranquil Ebro Valley to the dramatic Molinar River gorge and the majestic peaks of the Montes Obarenes. You'll find lush beech and pine forests, especially around Pico Humión, and spectacular canyons carved by the Ebro River.

Q: Are there any hikes featuring waterfalls in Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina?
A: Yes, the Molinar River gorge, located between the villages of Tobera and Ranera, is famous for its series of picturesque waterfalls. A well-marked circular path along the Molinar River in Tobera offers various viewpoints to appreciate their beauty. You can explore this area on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobera Waterfalls Viewpoint – View of Frías loop from Ranera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or viewpoints to see while hiking?
A: The highest point, &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pico_Humi%C3%B3n" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Humión&lt;/a&gt;, offers expansive panoramic views, allowing sightseers on clear days to observe mountains across ten different provinces. The Molinar River gorge with its waterfalls is another significant natural feature. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4244321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humion&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4294908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cueto&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain hikes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, the ascent to Pico Humión offers demanding routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045958731/humion-and-mancubo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humión and Mancubo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.9 km route that ascends towards Pico Humión, involving significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easier hiking options?
A: Yes, the region offers easier options suitable for families. The well-marked circular path along the Molinar River in Tobera is a popular and accessible choice, with shorter options available. There are 18 easy routes in the area, providing gentle walks through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045958731/humion-and-mancubo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humión and Mancubo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10513870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frías – Hanging Houses of Frías loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the historic village of Frías.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina?
A: The diverse ecosystems and varied terrain make hiking enjoyable across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, while summer is ideal for higher altitude trails. The lush beech and pine forests provide scenic beauty throughout the year.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, the municipality includes charming villages like Cubilla, Ranera, and Valderrama, each with unique character and historical structures such as traditional churches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10513870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frías – Hanging Houses of Frías loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through the historic village of Frías and its unique architecture.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Partido De La Sierra En Tobalina?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 140 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys and waterfalls to challenging mountain ascents, and the well-maintained network of paths suitable for various ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the forested areas?
A: Absolutely. The region is adorned with lush beech forests, especially at the base of Pico Humión, and extensive pine forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10513847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humion – TalasDelSomo loop from Peña San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate path that specifically explores the forested slopes around Pico Humión, offering a rich woodland experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided here, the charming villages within the municipality, such as Cubilla, Ranera, and Valderrama, often serve as convenient starting points for many routes and typically offer local parking options for visitors.

faqs.question: Where does the Ascent to Humión trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail begins in the charming village of Cubilla de la Sierra, which is one of the highest villages in Burgos province. As a starting point, it offers an attractive glimpse of traditional architecture. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's generally possible to find parking within or near such villages, especially given its isolated nature.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route. Starting and ending in Cubilla de la Sierra, it allows hikers to experience varied scenery during both the ascent and descent, including a beautiful and very aerial ridge on the way down.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this hike?
faqs.answer: This is considered a difficult hike, requiring good to very good physical fitness and sure-footedness. While the ascent from Cubilla de la Sierra is noted as the most accessible way to reach Humión, it still presents a significant challenge with approximately 630 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Ascent to Humión?
faqs.answer: The trail starts on a track from Cubilla de la Sierra. After a small wooded area, it opens up, and you'll follow a marked trail ascending steeply towards the collado separating Humión from Pico Flor. The path is generally evident, but some sections, particularly during the descent, can be earthy or muddy, requiring careful footing.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is renowned for its stunning panoramic views. You'll pass several notable points, including the PeñaSanPedro viewpoint, the Mancubo and Peña Horcada summits, and the Estrechon de Sarlota gorge. The summit of Humion itself, marked by a large metal cross, offers spectacular views across the Tobalina Valley and the Ebro River, with the Sierra de Árcena in the background. On clear days, you might even see mountains from up to ten different provinces.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, which features a mix of Atlantic and Mediterranean vegetation. While the information doesn't specify an ideal season, generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes for hiking in this region, avoiding the extremes of summer heat or winter snow, which could make a difficult trail even more challenging.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will hike along parts of the Sendero del Mancubo, Cubilla y hayedos del Humión, Sendero del Humión, and Bosques y Cumbres de Miraveche.

faqs.question: Is the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Natural Park generally welcomes visitors, specific regulations regarding dogs (e.g., leash requirements) can vary. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers, especially in a natural park setting.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As a natural park, the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park typically does not require entrance fees or permits for general hiking access. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially if planning any specific activities or camping.

faqs.question: What should I know about the descent from Humión?
faqs.answer: The descent often follows a "beautiful and very aerial ridge" to the west of the summit. While offering stunning views, be prepared for sections that can be earthy or muddy, especially after rain, requiring sure-footedness. This part of the circular route adds to the trail's challenging nature.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park is known for its rich biodiversity, influenced by both Atlantic and Mediterranean vegetation. While specific sightings are never guaranteed, the park is home to various species. Keep an eye out for birds of prey soaring above the peaks and other local fauna that thrive in this diverse environment.
